'''Barry Ward''' (born 13 January 1971 in [[New South Wales]]), is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s.

He played for the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] for nine seasons between 1990-1993 and 1997–2001, the [[Illawarra Steelers]] for one season in 1994, [[Sydney Roosters|Eastern Suburbs]] for one season in 1995 and [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] as a {{rlp|pr}} and in the {{rlp|sr}}. He played over 100 first grade games during his long NRL career.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/ |title=Home |publisher=Rugby League Project}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.roosters.com.au/about/past-players/|title=Men's Honour Roll|website=Sydney Roosters|access-date=23 January 2019|archive-date=27 March 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190327090103/https://www.roosters.com.au/about/past-players/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

==Playing career==
In 1998, Ward was fined for racial vilification towards [[Anthony Mundine]].<ref name="Profile">{{cite news|last=Pramberg|first=Bernie|url=http://www.news.com.au/couriermail/story/0,23739,25538251-10389,00.html|title=Anthony Mundine says Paul Gallen deserved 10-week knockout for alleged racist remarks|date=2008-06-16|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090527093047/http://www.news.com.au/couriermail/story/0,23739,25538251-10389,00.html|archive-date=27 May 2009|work=[[The Courier-Mail]]}}</ref>

Ward played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at prop forward in their [[2002 Super League Grand Final]] victory against the [[Bradford Bulls]]. Having won [[Super League VI]], St Helens contested the [[2003 World Club Challenge]] against 2002 NRL Premiers, the [[Sydney Roosters]]. Ward played from the interchange bench in Saints' 38–0 loss.

==Post playing==
In 2008, Ward became a member of the Canterbury Bulldogs Football Club board.<ref name="Ward">{{cite news 
|title=Canterbury League Club Increases Bulldogs Funding 
|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=32280 
|publisher=[[World of Rugby League]] 
|date=2008-05-15 
|url-status=dead 
|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090215103904/http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=32280 
|archive-date=15 February 2009 
}}</ref> Ward relinquished membership of the board in late 2011 when he was named coach of the NSW Cup team.<ref>Bulldogs Media (2011). http://nswrl.com.au/default.aspx?s=article-display&id=44826 {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120530014732/http://www.nswrl.com.au/default.aspx?s=article-display&id=44826 |date=30 May 2012 }}. Retrieved 1 November 2011</ref>
